Benhadad sent Ahab a very insulting demand. Ahab sent a very shameful submission; sin brings people into such difficult situations by taking them out of God’s protection. If God does not rule us, our enemies will: guilt makes people lose heart and become cowards. Ahab became desperate. People will give up their most pleasant things, those they love the most, to save their lives; yet they lose their souls rather than give up any pleasure or interest to prevent it. Here is one of the wisest sayings that Ahab ever spoke, and it is a good lesson for everyone. It is foolish to boast about any day to come, since we do not know what it may bring forth. Apply this to our spiritual conflicts. Peter fell because of self-confidence. Happy is the person who stays alert.

The proud Syrians were beaten, and the despised Israelites were conquerors. The orders of the proud, drunken king confused his troops and prevented them from attacking the Israelites. Those who are most secure are commonly least courageous. Ahab killed the Syrians with a great slaughter. God often makes one wicked person a scourge to another.

Those around Benhadad advised him to change his position. They assumed that it was not Israel, but Israel’s gods, that beat them; but they spoke very ignorantly of Jehovah. They supposed that Israel had many gods, to whom they ascribed limited power within a certain district; thus the Gentiles were vain in their imaginations concerning God. The greatest wisdom in worldly concerns is often united with the most contemptible folly in the things of God.

This encouragement sinners have to repent and humble themselves before God: Have we not heard that the God of Israel is a merciful God? Have we not found him so? That is gospel repentance, which flows from an understanding of God’s mercy in Christ; there is forgiveness with him. What a change this is! The most haughty in prosperity are often most abject in adversity; an evil spirit will thus affect a person in both these conditions. There are those on whom, like Ahab, success is ill bestowed; they do not know how to serve God or their generation, or even their own true interests with their prosperity: Let favor be shown to the wicked, yet he will not learn righteousness. The prophet designed to reprove Ahab by a parable. If a good prophet were punished for sparing his friend and God’s when God said, Smite, of much sorer punishment should a wicked king be thought worthy, who spared his enemy and God's, when God said, Smite. Ahab went to his house, heavy and displeased, not truly penitent, or seeking to undo what he had done amiss; every way out of humor, despite his victory. Alas! many that hear the glad tidings of Christ are busy and remain until the day of salvation is gone.
